items.rationale: The house's highest-conviction bet: declared to have durable product-market fit, cheapest way to send a dollar (<1s, <1¢), volume rivaling/exceeding Visa, organic use uncorrelated with trading, and a vector to sustain USD dominance via Treasury demand. GENIUS Act gave legal clarity.
items.age_note: bullish since 2024 State of Crypto; conviction has only intensified through 2026

citations.quote: Stablecoins are to money what the internet was to fax machines.
Stablecoins have found product-market fit, now rivaling the world's largest payment networks in transaction volume.
Stablecoins have done $33 trillion in transaction volume in the last 12 months

items.rationale: Reframed from niche to the single most important moat and a near-dealbreaker for institutional adoption. Thesis: privacy creates chain lock-in ('bridging secrets is hard') and winner-take-most dynamics. Backed with repeat Seismic investments and enthusiasm for Aztec/Aleo/Zcash ZSAs.
items.age_note: emerged as a top theme in late 2025; earlier corpus treated it as one feature among many

citations.quote: Privacy will be the most important moat in crypto.
the lack of privacy onchain is one of the biggest sources of friction, if not a dealbreaker, for many institutions

items.rationale: Seen as a defining convergence: crypto as the only viable payment rail and guardrail layer for autonomous agents (x402), plus verification, decentralized compute, and data markets. Heavy deployment: Catena, Yupp, Poseidon, Ambient, Gensyn, EigenCloud.
items.age_note: thesis since 2024 ('AI needs blockchain'); agent-payments angle sharpened through 2025-2026

citations.quote: Crypto might be the only viable payment rail for AI agents.
x402 means micropayments might finally be a real business model online

items.rationale: Framed as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies, bringing user-generated services to the physical world (energy, telecom, transport, robotics). Now an explicit GP investment focus area; Helium cited as proof of scale.

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Rising DEX/CEX share, perps and lending moving onchain, and a named GP focus. Viewed as having the simplest value proposition ('people can make more money') and therefore unstoppable; protected as credibly-neutral infrastructure.

citations.quote: I'll focus on investing in DeFi, DePIN, and the infrastructure powering those applications
20% of spot crypto trading now happens in DeFi

items.rationale: Strong push that the era of speculative pie-chart tokens is over; network tokens should capture protocol fees via programmatic buyback-burn (EIP-1559, UniStaker), enabling true economic independence. Uniswap fee switch held up as the template.
items.age_note: value-accrual stance held since 2024; made concrete and 'now possible' once regulatory clarity arrived in 2025

citations.quote: The era of network tokens with decentralized onchain revenue is here.
network tokens should have revenue.

items.rationale: Hayekian information-aggregation engines superior to polls; a breakout category in 2025. Co-led Kalshi Series D with planned crypto integrations; sees them as a trust-replacement for failing institutions.

citations.quote: This is the right moment for prediction markets.
Volume has grown 8x since July, to more than $1B per week.

items.rationale: Wall Street and Global 2000 adoption now the dominant narrative; CLARITY/Project Crypto seen as accelerating (not prohibiting) tokenized securities. $30B RWA already onchain; founders advised to adopt an enterprise sales playbook.

citations.quote: Wall Street is adopting blockchains as a schelling point to upgrade their atrophying core ledgers.
$30B in real-world assets are tokenized onchain

items.rationale: Jolt zkVM celebrated as a breakthrough now powering LayerZero's Zero L1; sum-check + multilinear polynomial techniques seen as the long-haul winners. Excitement is real but explicitly tempered by production-readiness caveats (see concerns).

citations.quote: Jolt is a technical breakthrough, and now core infrastructure powering the new L1 from @LayerZero_Core.

items.rationale: Persistently dismissed as ownership of nothing, redistributive gambling with no inherent value; blamed for diverting the industry toward extraction over value creation under bad regulation.
items.age_note: consistent stance 2024-2025; not heavily restated after spring 2025 but never reversed

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.
memecoins have no inherent value. That's the whole point.

items.rationale: Concern that diluting CLARITY's control-based framework and transfer restrictions creates incentives to fake decentralization and dump on retail, and that handing the SEC/CFTC broad discretion (post-Tornado Cash) is dangerous.
items.age_note: active concern through late 2025; CLARITY itself viewed favorably, the Senate simplification critically

citations.quote: The undermining of CLARITY's transfer restriction framework creates short-term incentives to circumvent decentralization and dump on retail.
The Senate's approach is simple because it punts all the hard stuff to regulators.

items.rationale: Structural worry that PBS and order-flow advantages create 'big-get-bigger' builder duopolies and predatory MEV, undermining decentralization. Addressed via Jito BAM investment and encrypted order flow, but treated as unsolved.

citations.quote: more than 85% the blocks are produced by just the biggest two block builders
BAM... is to address some of the more insidious forms of MEV.

items.rationale: Strong objection to regulating autonomous protocols or baking dynamic risk-scoring/whitelists into them; argued this destroys credible neutrality and makes DeFi permissioned and balkanized. A protocol can be neutral OR a gatekeeper, not both.

citations.quote: Regulating autonomous blockchain protocols is not a viable solution — it would kill the technology.

items.rationale: Recurring frustration that preventable losses keep happening; calls for rate-limited minting/withdrawals, admin time-delays, ERC-20R recovery, formal verification, and a first-principles, holistic security model.

citations.quote: The Resolv issue could have been almost completely mitigated. We've had to relearn this lesson so many times.

items.rationale: Skepticism toward treasury-management vehicles for less-mature tokens: unsolved disclosure/fraud tension, sub-NAV trading, Gary Plastic issues, and SPAC-craze froth likely to draw lawsuits.
items.age_note: raised mid-2025, escalated to predicting litigation by late 2025

citations.quote: The class action lawsuits over DATs are coming.
I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are... controlling these public vehicles

items.rationale: Treated as real but over-hyped on timeline: a CRQC before ~2035 is judged highly unlikely. Genuine near-term worry is Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later for encryption and Bitcoin's slow governance / vulnerable addresses; bugs remain the bigger near-term risk.
items.age_note: the explicit posture is 'match urgency to actual threats' — concerned but anti-panic

citations.quote: The clearest near-term risk is Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later (HNDL)
Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.

items.rationale: Acknowledged as not yet working: too-small user base (~50M daily onchain), harder to build than finance apps, and warped by prior hostile regulation into extraction/hype. Conviction it eventually works once stablecoins bring users on, but a present-tense disappointment.
items.age_note: framed as 'too early / distorted,' not a thesis reversal

citations.quote: Consumer crypto has not yet had a real shot.
The size of the market is still small.

items.rationale: A live, unresolved research concern: L2 scaling may divert TVL and liquidity off L1 such that more L2 activity could reduce ETH price, exposing a gap between 'Ethereum the chain' and 'ether the asset.' Voiced as open question, not house verdict.
items.age_note: surfaced sharply in Jan 2026 research discussion; contrasts with earlier unqualified ETH bullishness

citations.quote: an increase in L2 activity can in fact reduce ether prices.
what is the value in the asset separate from the chain ether?

items.rationale: Praised for pragmatic engineering culture and delivering global-scale financial markets — fast, sub-cent, sub-second; leading stablecoin and DeFi growth. Reinforced by Jito (JTO) investment and BAM for fair markets.

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers who want to build a platform
transactions for under a penny in under a second on Solana

items.rationale: Held up as the model network token: a closed-loop where protocol fees are captured by the token via buyback-and-burn, with optional minting to fund growth. Repeatedly cited as the clearest 'network token, not company token.'
items.age_note: excitement crystallized around the Nov 2025 fee-switch / DUNI proposal

citations.quote: Uniswap will finally become a "closed loop" network token

items.rationale: Conviction expressed via capital: +$70M EIGEN (verifiable computing for AI), +$55M ZRO with LayerZero's Jolt-powered Zero L1, and JTO for fair Solana market structure — all locked up, signaling structural bets on credibly-neutral infrastructure.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.
partnering with... team to help Solana become the dominant venue for Internet Capital Markets.

items.rationale: Still the canonical decentralized network token with automatic EIP-1559 burn capturing value; the legal/economic anchor for the whole 'network token ≠ security' thesis. Enthusiasm is now qualified by the L2 value-accrual debate (see concerns).
items.age_note: long unqualified bull; cooled somewhat by Jan 2026 L1-vs-L2 research doubts

citations.quote: I think ETH is already doing (fully automatic) buybacks via the EIP-1559 burn.

items.rationale: Respected as native asset of the most secure, decentralized network — distinct from a memecoin — and a store of value. Held with caveats on quantum-vulnerable addresses and slow governance.

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics

items.rationale: The team's recurring cautionary archetype: economically a profits interest in FTX with all the risks of a security despite no formal rights. Used to argue control-based tests and that company-backed tokens should be regulated as securities.
items.age_note: used as the canonical bad-token example consistently from 2024 through 2026

citations.quote: Ultimately, FTT was a security.
That token would be a profits interest in the company — its risk profile would look identical to that of a security.

items.rationale: Classed as speculative, inherently valueless, and often disguised company-backed tokens; flagged as exposing retail to information-asymmetry harms and as the wrong thing for the industry to optimize for.
items.age_note: stated through early-mid 2025; not re-litigated recently but stance unchanged

citations.quote: What's the purpose of using a company's brand for a memecoin?
some self-described "memecoins" are definitely company-backed tokens

items.rationale: Skepticism that L2 native tokens become meaningful risk assets, and worry that a single dominant L2 (e.g., Base-scale) accrues market power and could ultimately defect from the L1 — bad for ETH and for L2-token value alike.

citations.quote: we don't see the native layer 2 tokens being very sort of successful in terms of becoming the main risk asset

risk_regime.rationale: The house is emphatically constructive: crypto has 'grown up' into an institutional/enterprise era with $4T market cap, stablecoin PMF (>$1T monthly volume), reliable cheap infrastructure (3,400+ tps), and — decisively — regulatory clarity (GENIUS passed; CLARITY advancing; SEC/CFTC constructive). The dominant register is 'never been more optimistic.' Risk-on is tempered, not contradicted, by execution-level worries: getting market-structure law right, security/hacks, MEV centralization, and an honest admission that consumer crypto and some token value-accrual questions remain unproven. Posture is structural conviction with tactical caution, not euphoria.

notes: Corpus is multi-voice (legal/decentralization, research/cryptography, economics, data, investing) treated as one house. Where voices diverge it is by domain, not contradiction — e.g., legal authors drive the network-token/decentralization thesis while research voices supply the most pointed internal challenges (ETH-vs-L2 value accrual; MEV; zkVM readiness; quantum hype), which I logged as genuine concerns rather than smoothing them over. Strongest recency signal across late-2025/early-2026: stablecoins + privacy + enterprise/Wall Street adoption + onchain-revenue network tokens are ascendant; the speculative/memecoin and offshore-foundation eras are explicitly declared over. Notable evolution: 'decentralization' is being recast as 'absence of control / credible neutrality,' and one voice argued to drop the word entirely (2025-07-22). Conviction scores are intensity of stated enthusiasm/worry, not probability of payoff.
